purpose: Central orchestration container. Translates application-level config (APPL_DB) into ASIC-level SAI operations (ASIC_DB). Contains orchagent and all *mgrd/*syncd daemons. Acts as the coordination hub between the control plane (FRR, config management) and the forwarding plane (syncd/SAI/ASIC).

daemons.name: orchagent
daemons.role: Central state machine and orchestration engine. Subscribes to APPL_DB tables (ROUTE_TABLE, NEIGH_TABLE, VLAN_TABLE, ACL_TABLE, etc.), translates entries into SAI API calls, and writes resulting SAI object IDs into ASIC_DB. Manages a comprehensive set of Orch objects (RouteOrch, NeighOrch, PortOrch, AclOrch, etc.) each responsible for a sub-domain.

reads: APPL_DB: ROUTE_TABLE, NEIGH_TABLE, VLAN_TABLE, VLAN_MEMBER_TABLE, PORT_TABLE, LAG_TABLE, ACL_TABLE, MIRROR_SESSION_TABLE, TUNNEL_TABLE, BUFFER_PROFILE_TABLE

daemons.name: portmgrd
daemons.role: Watches CONFIG_DB PORT_TABLE for admin-state, MTU, speed, FEC changes. Applies them via Linux netlink (ip link set) and writes results to APPL_DB PORT_TABLE for orchagent to program into SAI.

daemons.name: intfmgrd
daemons.role: Manages L3 interface lifecycle. Watches CONFIG_DB INTF_TABLE for IP address assignments. Creates/deletes Linux kernel interfaces, programs IP addresses, writes to APPL_DB INTF_TABLE for orchagent.

daemons.name: vlanmgrd
daemons.role: Manages VLAN and VLAN membership lifecycle. Watches CONFIG_DB VLAN_TABLE and VLAN_MEMBER_TABLE. Creates Linux bridge devices for VLANs, enslaves member ports, and writes to APPL_DB for orchagent to create SAI VLAN/VLAN_MEMBER objects.

daemons.name: neighsyncd
daemons.role: Listens to Linux kernel netlink events for ARP/NDP neighbor state changes (RTM_NEWNEIGH, RTM_DELNEIGH). Writes neighbor entries into APPL_DB NEIGH_TABLE for orchagent to program into hardware via NeighOrch. Critical for L3 forwarding adjacency.

daemons.name: portsyncd
daemons.role: Monitors Linux kernel netlink for port link-state changes (RTM_NEWLINK). Translates kernel link-up/link-down events into APPL_DB PORT_TABLE oper_status updates that orchagent uses to track hardware port state.

daemons.name: nbrmgrd
daemons.role: Neighbor manager. Handles static neighbor (ARP/NDP) entries from CONFIG_DB STATIC_NEIGHBOR_TABLE. Installs them into the Linux kernel via ip neigh replace, from where neighsyncd picks them up for APPL_DB.

daemons.name: tunnelmgrd
daemons.role: Manages VXLAN and IP tunnel lifecycle. Watches CONFIG_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE, VXLAN_EVPN_NVO_TABLE. Creates Linux VXLAN netdevices, writes tunnel state to APPL_DB for orchagent VxlanOrch.

daemons.name: buffermgrd
daemons.role: Manages dynamic buffer configuration. Computes per-port buffer profiles (headroom, shared buffer) based on cable length and speed. Writes buffer profiles to APPL_DB for orchagent to program into SAI buffer/queue objects.

daemons.name: coppmgrd
daemons.role: Control Plane Policing manager. Reads trap group and rate-limit config from CONFIG_DB, translates to APPL_DB COPP_TABLE entries that orchagent programs via SAI hostif trap groups.

daemons.name: sflowmgrd
daemons.role: sFlow manager. Monitors CONFIG_DB SFLOW_TABLE and SFLOW_SESSION_TABLE, programs sFlow sampling rates into APPL_DB for orchagent to configure SAI samplepacket sessions.

daemons.name: natmgrd
daemons.role: NAT manager. Translates CONFIG_DB NAT_TABLE/NAPT_TABLE entries into APPL_DB for orchagent NatOrch to program SAI NAT entries. Manages NAT zone and binding lifecycle.

daemons.name: macsecmgrd
daemons.role: MACsec manager. Handles MACsec profile and port configuration from CONFIG_DB. Interfaces with the wpa_supplicant MKA daemon and writes MACsec SA state to STATE_DB. Programs SAI MACsec objects via APPL_DB.

key_data_flows.path: FRR zebra -> FPM socket (TCP 2620) -> fpmsyncd (bgp container) -> APPL_DB:ROUTE_TABLE -> orchagent:RouteOrch -> ASIC_DB:ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY -> syncd -> SAI -> ASIC
key_data_flows.notes: Any break in this chain stops route programming. Check each hop independently. fpmsyncd runs in the bgp container, not swss; only the orchagent half of the chain lives in this container.

key_data_flows.path: Kernel ARP resolution -> neighsyncd (netlink RTM_NEWNEIGH) -> APPL_DB:NEIGH_TABLE -> orchagent:NeighOrch -> ASIC_DB -> syncd -> SAI -> ASIC
key_data_flows.notes: If neighbors are in APPL_DB but not in hardware, suspect orchagent or syncd. If not in APPL_DB, suspect neighsyncd.

key_data_flows.path: config_db.json / config load -> CONFIG_DB:VLAN_TABLE -> vlanmgrd -> Linux bridge -> APPL_DB:VLAN_TABLE -> orchagent:VlanOrch -> ASIC_DB -> syncd -> SAI -> ASIC
key_data_flows.notes: VLANs must exist in both Linux bridge and ASIC. Linux-only = no SAI programming; ASIC-only impossible via normal path.

key_data_flows.path: CONFIG_DB:PORT_TABLE -> portmgrd -> ip link set + APPL_DB:PORT_TABLE -> orchagent:PortOrch -> ASIC_DB -> syncd -> SAI -> ASIC
key_data_flows.notes: MTU, speed, FEC, admin-state changes flow through portmgrd. If port is up in Linux but not in ASIC, check orchagent PortOrch.

warm_restart.mechanism: orchagent saves RID (Router Interface/Object ID) table to ASIC_DB:RIDTABLE before exit. On restart, it reconciles existing ASIC state with APPL_DB entries instead of reprogramming everything, minimizing traffic disruption.
warm_restart.caveat: Warm restart does not survive syncd restart. If syncd also restarts, full reprogramming occurs.

restart_impact: Brief traffic disruption (seconds to tens of seconds depending on table size). All APPL_DB -> ASIC_DB state is reprogrammed from scratch unless warm_restart is enabled. With warm restart, ASIC state is retained and orchagent reconciles deltas only. Routes, neighbors, VLANs, ACLs are all temporarily absent from hardware during cold restart.

restart_commands.orchagent_only: docker exec swss supervisorctl restart orchagent
restart_commands.warm_restart: sudo warm-reboot (system-level) or docker exec swss supervisorctl restart swss (container warm restart if enabled)

symptoms: All routes disappear from hardware simultaneously
supervisorctl shows orchagent in FATAL state
swss container repeatedly restarting (systemctl status shows frequent restart timestamps)
causes: SAI library assertion or segfault during SAI API call
OOM killer terminating orchagent under memory pressure
Corrupt or unexpected ASIC_DB state on startup (stale entries from prior crash)
Scale limit hit -- orchagent exceeds max SAI objects and SAI returns error that triggers assert
diagnosis: dmesg | grep -i 'oom\|killed\|orchagent' -- check for OOM kill
ls -lt /var/core/*.core -- check for recent core files
redis-cli -n 1 dbsize -- check ASIC_DB size for scale issues
docker exec syncd tail -50 /var/log/syncd.log -- SAI errors often surface in syncd log first
resolution: For SAI crash: may require vendor SAI library update
For OOM: reduce table scale or add swap, check for memory leak with 'show processes memory'
For stale ASIC_DB: 'redis-cli -n 1 FLUSHDB' then restart swss (WARNING: causes full reprogramming)
For scale: reduce FIB/MAC/ACL entries to within supported limits

common_issues.issue: Routes in APPL_DB but missing from ASIC_DB
symptoms: show ip route shows routes present
causes: orchagent RouteOrch backpressure -- ASIC_DB write queue full
Next-hop not yet resolved (NeighOrch waiting for ARP reply)
SAI returned error on route add (scale limit, unsupported prefix length)
orchagent stuck in retry loop on a different object type blocking route processing
diagnosis: Check next-hop: redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'NEIGH_TABLE:Ethernet48:x.x.x.x' -- must exist before route programs
redis-cli -n 1 hgetall 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEXT_HOP:oid:*' | grep x.x.x.x -- verify NH object exists in ASIC_DB
Check orchagent memory: show processes memory | grep orchagent
resolution: If NH missing: resolve ARP/NDP issue first (ping next-hop, check arp table)
If SAI error: check syncd log for SAI return code; may need vendor escalation
If backpressure: reduce route churn rate; check for route flapping in BGP

common_issues.issue: Port stuck operationally down despite physical link up
symptoms: Transceiver shows Rx/Tx power in range (show interfaces transceiver)
show interfaces status shows port as 'D' (down)
Physical LED shows link on some platforms
portsyncd log shows no RTM_NEWLINK event
causes: portmgrd failed to apply speed/FEC config -- kernel interface in wrong state
orchagent PortOrch did not complete port initialization sequence
SAI port creation failed during syncd initialization
Interface held down administratively in CONFIG_DB but config not reflected in kernel
diagnosis: ip link show <interface> -- check Linux kernel state vs SAI state
redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' -- check APPL_DB oper_status and admin_status
resolution: config interface startup <interface> -- ensure admin up in CONFIG_DB
If speed/FEC mismatch: config interface speed <interface> <speed> && config interface fec <interface> <fec_mode>
If persistent: docker exec swss supervisorctl restart portmgrd && portsyncd

common_issues.issue: fpmsyncd not writing routes to APPL_DB (swss side)
symptoms: FRR 'show ip route' has routes, 'show bgp summary' established
No traffic forwarding despite BGP up
fpmsyncd log shows connection errors or silent
causes: fpmsyncd (runs in bgp container) cannot connect to APPL_DB in database container
FPM socket between zebra and fpmsyncd broken
fpmsyncd process crashed in bgp container (check there, not swss)
diagnosis: This issue originates in the bgp container -- see bgp-container.json
docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd

scale_considerations.max_routes: Platform-dependent. orchagent tracks route count against SAI capacity. Check 'show system resources' or vendor SAI limits.
scale_considerations.max_neighbors: Platform-dependent. Neighbor table exhaustion causes orchagent to drop new ARP entries.
scale_considerations.max_acl_entries: Per-ASIC limit. orchagent AclOrch returns error on overflow; check orchagent.log for 'ACL table entry create failed'.
scale_considerations.memory_pressure: orchagent holds full copies of all programmed tables in memory. On high-scale deployments, RSS can exceed 1GB. Monitor with 'show processes memory | grep orchagent'.
